rationale: The muted slate/olive/brown palette and architectural cross-section aesthetic clash hard with Mob Control's vibrant purple/cyan/yellow stylized cartoon DNA, and the "secret bunker reveal" hook has no natural bridge to cannon-aiming, multiplier gates, or mob growth — forcing the connection would feel like a bait-and-switch that hurts IPM quality. Audience overlap is only partial (curiosity-bait pulls broad casual viewers but skews older/female-leaning vs. the game's 18-35 male-skew). Recommend skipping unless we can radically retheme it as a "cross-section of an enemy base you're about to destroy," which is essentially a different archetype.
